verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (Cysteine-Rich Secretory Protein 1) is primarily a secreted protein found in the epididymis and seminal plasma. It is secreted without a transmembrane anchor. However, CRISP1 is documented to bind to the sperm surface via a tight, wash-resistant interaction with specific receptors/binding partners on the sperm plasma membrane outer leaflet during epididymal maturation, remaining stably surface-associated through fertilization events. This stable surface attachment is cell-type and developmental-state restricted (epididymal sperm), qualifying as contextual rather than constitutive surface expression across cell types.
